timeline_highlights.title: Supreme Court Upholds State Bans on Transgender Athletes in Sports
timeline_highlights.summary: The US Supreme Court has upheld state laws that prohibit transgender women and girls from competing in female sports, allowing states to enforce these bans. This decision overturns previous lower court rulings that favored transgender athletes in cases from West Virginia and Idaho. The ruling has significant implications for LGBTQ rights and the participation of transgender individuals in school and college athletics.

timeline_highlights.title: Supreme Court Upholds Birthright Citizenship, Rejects Trump Limits
timeline_highlights.summary: The Supreme Court has upheld the principle of birthright citizenship, rejecting attempts to impose limits proposed by Donald Trump. This decision has sparked celebrations among supporters outside the court, highlighting the significance of citizenship rights in the ongoing national debate. The ruling reinforces the legal framework that grants citizenship to individuals born on U.S. soil, regardless of their parents' immigration status.

timeline_highlights.title: Supreme Court Eliminates Limits on Party Campaign Spending
timeline_highlights.summary: The Supreme Court ruled 6-3 to eliminate restrictions on how much national political party committees can spend in coordination with federal candidates, citing First Amendment protections. This decision overturns longstanding campaign finance rules, allowing for unlimited spending in such arrangements. The ruling has significant implications for the financing of political campaigns in the United States.

Two-seat Cessna aircraft was being used for short flight experience when it crashed in Ongar on Tuesday, police say

Two people have died after a light aircraft on a “short flight experience” crashed in a field near Ongar in Essex, police have said.

The two-seat Cessna plane crashed in a field off Mill Lane, Ongar, on Tuesday after taking off from North Weald airfield about seven miles west, Essex police said. The two people onboard have not been formally identified.
